description: The hard fibrous material forming trunk and branches of trees; living material transformed for building. 'Wolea' names what trees give—the warm, working material of shelter and craft.
sound_symbolism: The word warms with life. 'Wo' opens with organic warmth, tree essence. 'Le' lives with cellular structure. Then 'a' opens to grain and growth rings.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'wolea' (the wood). Material from trees for building, carving, burning. Related to 'shiro' (tree), its living source.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Renewable resource—sustainably harvested wood as carbon-storing building material.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wood built civilization—homes, tools, ships, the renewable material of human shelter.
